In C++, the code would be:

class Solution {
public:
    int numWays(int steps, int arrLen) {
        const int MOD = 1e9 +7;
        if (arrLen ==1) return 1;
        vector<int> prev(1,1);
        for(int i=1; i<=steps; ++i){
            int current_max = min(i, arrLen-1);
            vector<int> curr(current_max +1, 0);
            int prev_max = min(i-1, arrLen-1);
            for(int j=0; j<=current_max; ++j){
                int sum =0;
                if(j-1 >=0 && j-1 <= prev_max){
                    sum += prev[j-1];
                }
                if(j <= prev_max){
                    sum += prev[j];
                }
                if(j+1 <= prev_max){
                    sum += prev[j+1];
                }
                curr[j] = sum % MOD;
            }
            prev = curr;
        }
        return prev[0] % MOD;
    }
};

solution: 
```cpp
class Solution {
public:
    int numWays(int steps, int arrLen) {
        const int MOD = 1000000007;
        if (arrLen == 1) return 1;
        vector<int> prev(1, 1);
        for (int i = 1; i <= steps; ++i) {
            int current_max = min(i, arrLen - 1);
            vector<int> curr(current_max + 1, 0);
            int prev_max = min(i - 1, arrLen - 1);
            for (int j = 0; j <= current_max; ++j) {
                int sum = 0;
                if (j - 1 >= 0 && j - 1 <= prev_max) {
                    sum += prev[j - 1];
                }
                if (j <= prev_max) {
                    sum += prev[j];
                }
                if (j + 1 <= prev_max) {
                    sum += prev[j + 1];
                }
                curr[j] = sum % MOD;
            }
            prev = curr;
        }
        return prev[0] % MOD;
    }
};
```

efficiency: HIGH

time complexity: O(steps * min(steps, arrLen))

space complexity: O(min(steps, arrLen))

energy implications: LOW

explanation: The solution uses dynamic programming with a rolling array to efficiently track the number of ways to return to index 0 after each step. The approach ensures that we only consider valid moves and positions, optimizing both time and space complexity.
